steps.title: Mark the contrast
steps.content: The first sentence says few New Yorkers know Pollard's name. The next sentence says many have seen her work in a famous Broadway musical. The transition must introduce a contrast between being unknown by name and being widely seen through her work.

steps.title: Reject smooth connections
steps.content: A signals similarity, B signals a result, and C signals an example. None captures the contrast between Pollard's obscurity and the visibility of her makeup work. The answer is D.
